introduction:
The Smart Ration Card in Tamil Nadu is issued by the Civil Supplies and Consumer Protection Department under the Public Distribution System (TNPDS). It entitles eligible households to get essential commodities like rice, sugar, uniform commodities, and kerosene from Fair Price Shops (FPS).

Tamil Nadu successfully operates a Universal PDS (UPDS). The digital Smart Card replaces the old booklet system and serves as a major proof of identity and residence. Under state schemes, beneficiaries also receive Pongal gift hampers and women-centric cash assistance linked to their ration cards.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Smart Cards are provided FREE of cost by the Government
  • Processing time typically takes 30-65 days
  • Applying online via the TNPDS portal or App is the quickest method
  • Aadhaar linking is strictly mandatory for all family members
  • Family Head should preferably be the eldest female member
  • Toll-Free Helpline: 1967 or 1800-425-5901
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Smart Ration Card

Note: Scanned copies must be under 2MB for online upload. Carry the originals when visiting the TSO (Taluk Supply Office) for physically verifying and capturing biometric info.
Identity Proof (Mandatory)
  • Aadhaar Card (of the Head and ALL members)
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Driving Licence
  • Passport
Address Proof (in Tamil Nadu)
  • Recent Electricity Bill
  • LPG Gas Consumer Book
  • Property Tax Receipt / Agreement
  • Bank Passbook (front page with photo)
  • Telephone Bill
Other Documents
  • Passport-size photograph of the Head of Family
  • Income Certificate (if applying for specific subsidies)
  • Surrender Certificate / Name Deletion Certificate (if moving from another address)
Commodity Card Types
  • PHH (Rice Card): Priority Household - Rice and all commodities
  • AAY (Rice Card): Antyodaya Anna Yojana
  • NPHH (Sugar Card): Sugar and other items (except Rice)
  • NPHH-NC (No Commodity): Only for identity/address proof

Note: Card type can be converted periodically based on govt notifications.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via TNPDS Portal

The government strongly encourages online registration via the TNPDS Portal or the official mobile app for a paperless and transparent process.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Access the Official Portal
    Navigate to www.tnpds.gov.in. You can choose Tamil or English language.
  2. Click Smart Card Application
    Under the Citizen Services section, click "Smart Card Application" (புதிய மின்னணு அட்டை விண்ணப்பிக்க).
  3. Fill the Application Form
    Enter details of the family head, full Tamil Nadu residential address, and district/taluk information. Upload a passport-size photograph of the family head (less than 5MB).
  4. Add Family Members
    Click "Add Member" and enter names, Aadhaar details, relation to the head for each member. Aadhaar numbers must be correctly entered.
  5. Select Card Type and Upload Proof
    Choose the required commodity card type (Rice, Sugar, No Commodity). Attach address proof (PDF, JPG, or PNG format).
  6. Submit and Save Reference Number
    Declare that details are correct and click "Submit". Note down the Reference Number sent via SMS.
  7. Physical Verification
    A Revenue Inspector (RI) will visit your residence for verification within a few weeks.
Tip: Ensure details like gas connection information closely align with your proofs to avoid rejection.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply via e-Sevai Maiyam or Taluk Supply Office

If an online application is difficult to complete, citizens can visit a nearby e-Sevai Maiyam (CSC) or the local Taluk Supply Office (TSO).

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit e-Sevai Center/TSO
    Find your local CSC/e-Sevai center. Operates generally Monday – Saturday.
  2. Take Required Documents
    Ensure you carry original Aadhaar cards, gas booklets, address proofs, and photos of the family head.
  3. Application Assistance
    The operator will scan your documents and fill out the TNPDS application on your behalf via their portal.
  4. Pay Service Fee & Collect Receipt
    Pay the nominal convenience fee (e.g., ₹60) applicable at e-Sevai centers.
    Collect the system-generated Acknowledgment Slip bearing your Reference Number.
  5. Revenue Inspector Verification
    Similar to the online method, await physical verification at your registered address from the zone's RI.
  6. Smart Card Issue
    You will get an SMS when the card is approved and printed. Collect the physical Smart Card from the Taluk Office.
Processing Time: Depending on the taluk and verification speeds, receiving the physical card might take up to 2 months.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application Status

Visit www.tnpds.gov.in and click Smart Card Application Status. Enter your Reference Number to verify.

Download E-Card / App

Download the TNPDS mobile app (available on Android). You can digitally view your card, entitlements, and previous transactions.

Make Corrections

Address changes, adding/removing members, or surrendering the card can be done seamlessly online through the TNPDS portal via OTP authentication.

Grievance Redressal

If there's excessive delay or FPS issues, register a complaint online through TNPDS or call the toll-free number 1967.
